standard_variable.semantic_grain: one row per retained ICU stay exit-destination category

candidate_boundary_notice.reason: Amsterdam destination appears mostly as numeric local codes, so same-name approval is pending official code-dictionary review.
candidate_boundary_notice.approval_gate: candidate runtime evidence only until detailed review and owner approval close

database_mapping.source_grain: one Amsterdam admissions_core local ICU/MC admission row

identifier_normalization.hadm_id_role: not separately available in the current Amsterdam opening mapping

admin_demographic_build_translation.identifier_translation: emit subject_id from patientid and stay_id from admissionid; keep hadm_id null because no separate hospital admission id is currently available
admin_demographic_build_translation.value_normalization_translation: retain admissions_core.destination as local exit-destination labels; numeric codes are exposed as local_exit_destination_code_* pending dictionary adjudication
admin_demographic_build_translation.no_row_translation: rows with null source values are not emitted in this first candidate asset

notes: Build-first candidate mapping; owner approval is explicitly pending.
Amsterdam admissions_core.destination gives local ICU/MC exit or discharge destination context when non-null.
Amsterdam destination appears mostly as numeric local codes, so same-name approval is pending official code-dictionary review.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: Candidate output should be generated through the governed execution.py path and reviewed before approval.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Approval-facing outputs should be produced only through the governed execution.py path.
